text: When you find a bug in your code, the first step is to identify exactly what is going wrong. A systematic approach helps you avoid wasting time. Start by reading error messages carefully; they often point to the problem. Then, use debugging tools like breakpoints in your code editor to pause the program and inspect values step by step. Another useful technique is adding logging statements to print variable states at key points. By logging information, you can track how data changes during execution. This systematic method makes debugging less frustrating and more efficient, allowing you to fix errors quickly and improve your code.
